slides.title: Food Vocabulary
slides.content:
Let's learn words about food and cooking!

🥛 cream — a thick, white liquid from milk

🍬 sugar — sweet white powder

🧂 salt — makes food taste salty

🫗 pour — to make a liquid flow out of a container

🥣 mixture — two or more things mixed together
slides.tip: A mixture is when you combine things. A fruit salad is a mixture of different fruits!

slides.title: More Food Words
slides.content:
More useful words about food:

🥬 vegetables — lettuce, tomatoes, carrots, peppers

🥗 salad — a mixture of vegetables (or fruit)

🍝 pasta — spaghetti, macaroni, penne

🍎 fruit — apples, bananas, oranges, strawberries

We eat different food every day to stay healthy!

slides.title: Making Ice Cream at Home
slides.html:
Today, Tom and his mum made ice cream at home!

First, Tom poured some cream into a small plastic bag. He added sugar and closed the bag.

Then, Mum put ice and salt in a big bag. Tom put the small bag inside the big bag.

'Now shake it for ten minutes!' said Mum. Tom shook and shook the bags.

The salt made the ice very cold. The cold mixture froze the cream.

'Was it easy?' asked Mum. 'Yes, it was!' said Tom. 'And it is delicious!'

slides.title: Questions with Was / Were
slides.content:
To ask questions in the past with to be, put was or were at the beginning:

👤 Was it cold? — Yes, it was. / No, it wasn't.

👤 Was she happy? — Yes, she was. / No, she wasn't.

👥 Were they at school? — Yes, they were. / No, they weren't.

👥 Were you tired? — Yes, I was. / No, I wasn't.
slides.tip: Just flip the order! 'It was cold' becomes 'Was it cold?'

slides.title: Making Questions
slides.problem:
Turn these sentences into questions:

1. It was fun.
2. They were at the park.
3. She was hungry.
steps: 1️⃣ Move 'was' to the front → Was it fun?
2️⃣ Move 'were' to the front → Were they at the park?
3️⃣ Move 'was' to the front → Was she hungry?
slides.answer: 1. Was it fun? 2. Were they at the park? 3. Was she hungry?

slides.title: Short Answers with Was / Were
slides.content:
We answer yes/no questions with short answers:

❓ Was it easy?
✅ Yes, it was. / ❌ No, it wasn't.

❓ Were they happy?
✅ Yes, they were. / ❌ No, they weren't.

❓ Was he at home?
✅ Yes, he was. / ❌ No, he wasn't.
slides.tip: In short answers, we do NOT repeat the full sentence. Just say 'Yes, it was' not 'Yes, it was easy'.

slides.title: Answer the Questions
slides.problem:
Answer these questions with short answers:

1. Was the ice cream delicious? (yes)
2. Were the vegetables fresh? (yes)
3. Was the pasta cold? (no)
slides.answer: 1. Yes, it was. 2. Yes, they were. 3. No, it wasn't.

slides.title: The Cooking Class
slides.html:
Today the children had a cooking class. They made a salad with vegetables.

'Was the lettuce fresh?' asked the teacher. 'Yes, it was!' said Sara.

Then they made pasta with tomato sauce. It was a delicious mixture!

'Were the results good?' asked the teacher. 'Yes, they were!' said the children.

'Was the experiment fun?' 'Yes, it was great!'

Tom poured juice for everyone. It was the best class ever!

slides.title: Mixtures
slides.content:
A mixture is when you put two or more things together:

🥗 A salad is a mixture of vegetables.

🍝 Pasta with sauce is a mixture.

☕ Tea with milk and sugar is a mixture.

Some mixtures can be separated: you can pick fruit out of a fruit salad.

Some mixtures cannot be separated: you cannot take sugar out of water!

slides.title: Questions and Answers Practice
slides.problem:
Write questions and short answers:

1. The mixture / good → ___? Yes, ___.
2. The children / happy → ___? Yes, ___.
3. The salt / in the bag → ___? No, ___.
steps: 1️⃣ 'The mixture' is singular → Was the mixture good? Yes, it was.
2️⃣ 'The children' is plural → Were the children happy? Yes, they were.
3️⃣ 'The salt' is singular → Was the salt in the bag? No, it wasn't.
slides.answer: 1. Was the mixture good? Yes, it was. 2. Were the children happy? Yes, they were. 3. Was the salt in the bag? No, it wasn't.

slides.title: Let's Review!
points: 🍦 Food vocabulary: cream, sugar, salt, pour, mixture
🥗 More food: vegetables, salad, pasta, fruit
❓ Questions with was/were: put was/were at the beginning
✅ Short answers: Yes, it was. / No, it wasn't. / Yes, they were. / No, they weren't.
🥣 A mixture is two or more things combined together
🔄 Some mixtures can be separated, some cannot
